observations.dashboardShortStatus: Checking sources; available data is shown as it loads.
observations.dashboardShortBrief:
SPACE BRIEF

Space activity is only partially visible.

Falcon 9 Block 5 Transporter 17 (Dedicated SSO Rideshare) is next on the schedule, t-15h. Asteroid tracking is unavailable from NASA right now. Space weather remains quiet conditions at Kp 0.7. 10 people remain in orbit, while ISS position is unavailable.

ALERT
ALERT: Electron 2MeV Integral Flux exceeded 1000pfu

Satellite environment watch
Elevated high-energy electron activity can affect the satellite environment; minimal impact is expected for most users.

Jul 6, 2026, 7:50 AM EDT
ALERT
ALERT: Type II Radio Emission

Alert context
NOAA has issued a space-weather notice. Apollo has no additional impact signal from the alert text.

observations.issBody:
ISS STATUS

Normal Operations

The International Space Station is operating normally with 10 crew members aboard. The station is currently over the southern Indian Ocean at 423 km and traveling at 27,570 km/h. Crew are distributed across Crew-12 Dragon, Shenzhou 23, and Soyuz MS-28.

423 km altitude
27,570 km/h
Sunlit
Over the southern Indian Ocean

+
−
 Leaflet | © OpenStreetMap contributors

Position fix Jul 6, 2026, 11:58 AM EDT

CURRENT POSITION

the southern Indian Ocean

The station is moving at orbital speed while sunlit.

ALTITUDE
423 km
VELOCITY
27,570 km/h

ORBITAL SNAPSHOT

Over the southern Indian Ocean
Sunlit
15.5 orbits/day
4,524 km footprint
One orbit every 93 min

Orbit estimates use current altitude and velocity; sunlight and footprint come from the ISS position source.

LATITUDE
-17.9819
LONGITUDE
24.6530
